ksa_text: Knowledge of computer vision task taxonomy, including image classification, object detection, segmentation, pose estimation, and generation, and the architectures appropriate for each.

eu_ai_act.top_element_label: Transparency and provision of information to deployers

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain AI/ML Engineering KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.387) to justify inclusion for the VP Data Engineering. Without stronger framework alignment, this knowledge KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain knowledge KSA be expected in real-world VP Data Engineering roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Job market analysis and framework role definitions confirm AI/ML Engineering is not a standard competency expectation for VP Data Engineering. Edge-case exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence does not support including this edge-domain KSA in the VP Data Engineering competency profile. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Insufficient signal (score 0.387) and edge-domain placement (AI/ML Engineering) do not meet inclusion threshold for the VP Data Engineering. All passes support exclusion.
